back_translation: Meanwhile, when tens-of-thousands of the crowd had gathered, so that they were trampling one another, He began to speak to His disciples first: «Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
There is nothing that has been covered up which will not be revealed, and nothing that is hidden which will not become known.
Therefore, whatever you have spoken in the darkness will be heard in the light, and what you have whispered at the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed on the housetops.
I tell you, who are My friends, do not fear those who kill the body and after that are unable to do anything more.
But I will tell you whom you should fear: fear the One who, after the killing, has authority to throw into hell; I tell you, fear Him.
Are not five sparrows sold for two assarion coins? Yet not one of them will God forget.
But even every hair on your head has been numbered. Do not fear; you are worth more than many sparrows.
I tell you that everyone who acknowledges Me before men, the Son of Man will also acknowledge that one before the angels of God.
But whoever denies Me before men will be denied before the angels of God.
Everyone who speaks against the Son of Man will receive forgiveness, but the one who blasphemes the Holy Spirit will not receive forgiveness.
When they bring you to the synagogues, before officials and authorities, do not worry how you will defend the charge or what you will say.
For the Holy Spirit will teach you in that very hour what you ought to say.»
Someone in the crowd said to Him, «Teacher, please command my brother to divide the inheritance with me.»
He answered him, «Man, who appointed Me a judge or divider between you?»
Then He said to them, «Beware and guard yourselves against every kind of greed, for one's life does not consist in the abundance of the possessions he has.»
He told them a parable: «The land of a certain rich man produced very abundantly.
So he deliberated in his heart, 'What shall I do? For I have nowhere to store my crops.'
He thought, 'This is what I will do: I will tear down my barns and build them up bigger, and I will store all my grain and possessions there,
and I will say to my soul, "Soul, you have many good things stored up for many years; rest, eat, drink, and be merry!"'
But God said to him, 'You fool! This very night your soul is being demanded back from you — then the things you have accumulated, whose will they be?'
So it will be with the one who stores up possessions for himself but is not rich toward God.»
Then He said to His disciples, «For this reason I tell you, do not be anxious about life — what you will eat — or about the body — what you will wear.
For life is more important than food, and the body than clothing.
Consider the ravens: they do not sow or reap; they have no storeroom or barn, and God feeds them. How much more valuable you are than those birds!
Who among you, by being anxious, can extend his lifespan even by an hour?
If you cannot do this least thing, why are you anxious about the rest?
Consider how the lilies grow: they do not labor and do not spin thread; but I tell you, not even Solomon in all his splendor clothed himself as gloriously as one of these flowers.
If God so clothes the grass in the field, which exists today and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will He not clothe you so much more, O you of little faith!
And do not seek after what you will eat and drink, and do not be anxiously-suspended.
For all the people in the world who do not know God seek after these things, but your Father knows that you need these things.
But you, seek His kingdom, and these things will be added to you.
Little flock, do not fear, because your Father is pleased to give you the kingdom.
Sell your possessions and give to the poor; make for yourselves purses that do not grow old, an inexhaustible treasure in heaven, where thieves cannot come near and moths cannot destroy.
For where your treasure is, there your heart will also be.
Gird your waists and keep your lamps burning bright,
and you, be like people waiting for their master to return from a wedding, so that when he comes and knocks, they will open for him immediately.
Those slaves whom the master finds watching when he returns are blessed. I tell you the truth, he will gird himself and have them recline to eat, and he will come and serve them.
If he comes in the second watch or the third watch and finds them prepared thus, those slaves are blessed.
But know this: if the owner of the house knew at what hour the thief would come, he would not let his house be broken into.
You also, be ready, for at an hour you do not expect, the Son of Man will come.»
Peter asked, «Lord, are You telling this parable to us, or also to everyone?»
The Lord said, «Who then is the faithful and wise steward, whom the master will appoint to oversee his servants, to distribute food at the proper time?
That slave is blessed whom his master finds doing so when he returns.
I tell you the truth, he will appoint him to oversee all the master's possessions.
But if that slave says in his heart, 'My master is delayed in coming,' and begins to beat the menservants and maidservants, to eat and drink and become drunk,
the master of that slave will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not know, and will punish him severely and assign his place with those who are unfaithful.
The slave who knows his master's will but does not prepare or act according to his will, will be beaten with many blows.

But the slave who does not know, yet does things that deserve beating, will be beaten with few blows. Everyone who has received much, much will be required; and the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked of him.
I came to kindle fire upon the earth, and how I wish it were already lit!
But I have a baptism to receive, and I am greatly distressed until it is accomplished!
Do you think I came to bring peace on earth? I tell you, no, but rather division.
For from now on, five in one household will be divided: three against two, and two against three.
Father against son, son against father; mother against daughter, daughter against mother; mother-in-law against daughter-in-law, daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.»
Then He also said to the crowds, «When you see a cloud forming in the west, you say immediately, 'Rain is coming,' and it happens so.
When the south wind blows, you say, 'It will be hot,' and it is.
O hypocrites! You know how to analyze the appearance of earth and sky — then why do you not know how to analyze this present time?
And why do you not judge for yourselves what is right?

When you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, make every effort along the way to settle with him, so that he does not drag you to the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer put you in prison.
I tell you, you will by no means come out from there until you have paid the very last lepton coin.»
